nvidiarobotics.text:
Robots that only work in isolated cells won’t cut it for the next generation of smart factories. 🦾

At #NVIDIAGTC, hear from leading robotics innovators from @IDEALworksGmbH, @Universal_Robot, Vention, KUKA, and @theworldlabs are breaking out of these silos by integrating NVIDIA libraries, tools, and open frameworks to scale across real-world production environments.

Add to schedule 🔗 https://nvda.ws/4stoKBz
📅 Weds, March 18 | 11:00 AM PT

How do you bring real-time AI to robots and smart devices?

Join Chen Su at #NVIDIAGTC to discover how NVIDIA’s edge AI platform enables machines to perceive, reason, and act in real time without relying on the cloud, shaping the future of smart edge and autonomous robotics.

📅 Monday, March 16 | 4:00 p.m. PT.

Add to schedule 👉 https://nvda.ws/4b4cVL1

asimovinc.text: You can build your own humanoid at home. Asimov – Here be Dragons is now available for presale. $499 deposit, $15,000 target price. https://asimov.inc/diy-kit

RT by @asimovinc: For more context, look no further 👇
https://www.humanoidsdaily.com/news/asimov-launches-15-000-here-be-dragons-diy-humanoid-kit?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=author_promotion&utm_content=manual_post
RT by @asimovinc: Pre order is in for @asimovinc

RT by @asimovinc: 🐉 Here Be Dragons!

Asimov just launched the v1 “Here Be Dragons” DIY Humanoid Kit — $15,000 (near actual BOM cost).

Build your own open-source 1.20 m tall, 35 kg, 27+ DoF humanoid: 
• Modular snap-together design 
• 3D-printable parts + off-the-shelf components 
• Advanced RSU ankles, passive toes & zero-shot sim-to-real RL

Comes unassembled with full manual + build videos. 

Perfect for engineers, researchers & tinkerers who want to iterate fast.

RT by @asimovinc: Everyone can build their own humanoid robot.

After K-Sclae shut down, another open-source humanoid robot company, Asimov, released its robot DIY kit, allowing developers to assemble their own humanoid robots (1.2m, 35kg).

The kit includes: structural frame, actuators, motors, sensors, wiring harnesses, etc. It's like assembling a computer, though humanoid robots are much more difficult.

I checked the website; the price is $15,000, but shipping takes several months.

It's unclear whether people are willing to wait, or will ultimately opt for readily available humanoid robots (like the Unitree G1 and Booster K1 from China).

Hopefully, they will succeed.
RT by @asimovinc: Nuuuuuts - get your robot today
RT by @asimovinc: This is amazing guys

RT by @asimovinc: The Reachy Mini just got a big brother! 🤖

Open source humanoid DIY kit by @menloresearch 🤯
